page: Microsoft Azure D96as_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine belonging to the Dasv7 server family. It is powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor utilizing the x86_64 architecture. The server features 96 virtual CPUs mapped to 48 physical cores with 2.0 threads per core, operating at a clock speed of 4.5 GHz. It is equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per vCPU. The processor includes L1d cache of 48 KB, L2 cache of 1.0 MB, and L3 cache of 32 MB, alongside support for advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 vector extensions and cryptographic acceleration.

In benchmark evaluations, the D96as_v7 demonstrates top-tier performance across multiple domains. It achieves top-decile results in both single-core and multi-core stress-ng CPU tests, as well as in Passmark CPU and memory benchmarks. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with cached small-block reads and large-block writes registering in the top 10%, while uncached large-block reads perform in the top 25%. The server also excels in database operations, showing top-tier throughput in Redis SET operations and Passmark database tests. For language model workloads, the CPU-based LLM inference speeds reach top-tier performance for text generation across various model sizes, though prompt processing for larger models falls into the top 25% category.

A key hardware tradeoff is the complete absence of local storage, as the instance is configured with no local SSDs, requiring reliance on external Premium Storage options. Additionally, it does not include any complimentary public IPv4 addresses or GPU accelerators. Given its high-density 96-vCPU compute profile and balanced memory ratio, the D96as_v7 is highly cost-efficient for large-scale enterprise workloads that do not require local scratch space. It is well-suited for multi-threaded applications, high-throughput web serving, database management systems, and CPU-bound machine learning inference tasks.
description: The Microsoft Azure D96as_v7 is a general-purpose virtual machine from the Dasv7 family, powered by a dedicated AMD EPYC 9V45 processor. Operating at 4.5 GHz, it delivers 96 vCPUs across 48 physical cores and is paired with 384.0 GB of system memory, maintaining a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio. The instance lacks local storage and GPU acceleration, relying on external Premium Storage. Benchmarks place this server in the top 10% for both single-core and multi-core CPU performance, database operations, and memory write bandwidth. It is highly effective for compute-heavy multi-threaded applications, database hosting, high-throughput static web serving, and CPU-based LLM inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure D96as_v7 is a general-purpose instance featuring 96 vCPUs on an AMD EPYC 9V45 processor and 384 GB RAM. It delivers top-tier CPU and database performance with no local storage.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure D96as_v7 is a 96-vCPU general-purpose server with an AMD EPYC processor, 384 GB RAM, and top-tier multi-core benchmark performance.
tagline: A high-core, general-purpose virtual machine delivering top-tier AMD EPYC compute performance and balanced memory without local storage.
bullet_points: Powered by a 96-vCPU AMD EPYC 9V45 processor running at 4.5 GHz
Equipped with 384.0 GB of system memory (4.0 GB per vCPU)
Top-tier performance in multi-core CPU, database, and Redis benchmarks
Lacks local storage, requiring external Premium Storage volumes
Optimized for high-throughput web serving, databases, and CPU-based LLM inference
